LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CAROL LAM, AND, LAI LEE'S MOTION TO TRANSFER TO SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT THE ACTION ENTITLED FARMERS INSURANCE COMPANY, INC. VS. CAROL SUI MAN LAM, WILLIAM ZHOU AND LAI FONG LEE (RG 08 416808) OF THE ALAMEDA SUPERIOR COURT AND CONSOLIDATE WITH CASE CGC-07-468278 OF THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT, IN THE COUNTY SAN FRANCISCO, SUPERIOR COURT OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. THE TRANSFER WILL PROMOTE THE ENDS OF JUSTICE. BOTH CASES ARISE FROM THE SAME INCIDENT. THE COMPLAINT IN THE SAN FRANCISCO CASE AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IN THE ALAMEDA CASE ARE SUBSTANTIALLY SIMILAR. THESE ACTIONS ARE NOT COMPLEX. COMMON QUESTION OF LAW AND FACTS IS PREDOMINATING AND SIGNIFICANT: THE CONDITION OF THE PROPERTY BEFORE THE INCIDENT, DAMAGES, AND RESPONSIBILITY FOR PAYMENT OF THE DAMAGES. THE PARTIES, WITNESSES AND COUNSEL WILL NOT BE INCONVENIENCED BY THE TRANSFER. BOTH CASES ARE RECENTLY FILED. TRANSFER WOULD BE IN THE INTEREST OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY, AND ELIMINATE THE RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS, ORDERS, OR JUDGMENTS. COORDINATION INCREASES THE LIKELIHOOD OF SETTLEMENT OF THE ACTIONS. ORDER TRANSFERRING AND CONSOLIDATING THE MATTER, SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: PURSUANT TO ORAL REPRESENTATION FROM COUNSEL VIA TELEPHONE ON THE MORNING OF THE HEARING THAT ALL PARTIES INVOLVED AGREE TO CONTINUE THE MATTER, THE COURT RULES THAT THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 6, 2010. COUNSEL TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH LETTER CONFIRMING CONTINUANCE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF REGARDING RESCISSION, AND TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT RAISED. MEMORANDUM OF FIRE INSURANCE IS INITIALED ON ALL PAGES BY BOTH PLAINTIFF AND THE AGENT. THE TERM "UNREPAIRED DAMAGE" IS NOT EXPLAINED OR DEFINED. LAM DESCRIBED THE PROPERTY AS A "FIXER UPPER" WHICH SHOULD HAVE PROMPTED FURTHER INQUIRY AND INVESTIGATION. LAM'S DEPOSITION REFLECTS SOME LACK OF KNOWLEDGE ABOUT DAMAGE TO THE PROPERTY AND WHAT SHOULD BE FIXED. (PLAINTIFFS' UMP #53, 71.) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE AGENT'S DUTY TO INSPECT THE ENTIRE PROPERTY, (PLAINTIFFS' UMF 103, NEAL DEPOSITION) AND THE AGENT'S CHECKLIST (#105, 106). PHOTOS OF THE PROPERTY SHOW DAMAGE THE AGENT SHOULD HAVE REPORTED. (PLAINTIFFS' UMF 171, NEAL DEPOSITION.) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT'S INVESTIGATION WAS PROPER AND LED TO A "GENUINE DISPUTE." (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMERICAN SECURITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. FOR REASONS STATED IN RULING ON DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT RAISED WHETHER MID-CENTURY'S RESCISSION VALID. ASIC DOES NOT EXPLAIN WHY THE ISSUE OF WHETHER MID-CENTRURY IMPROPERLY RESCINDED ITS POLICY IS RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. EVEN IF MID-CENTURY IMPROPERLY RESCINDED, ASIC HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THIS WOULD HAVE AFFECT WELLS FARGO'S ACTIONS AND REQUIREMENTS WITH RESPECT TO RETAINING ASIC'S INSURANCE FOR PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS DO NOT SHOW THAT ASIC'S POLICY WAS CANCELLED OR OTHERWISE REMOVED BASED ON MID-CENTURY'S PURPORTEDLY IMPROPER RESCISSION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AMERICAN SECURITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: John N. Frye, Esq. Attorney at Law, (650) 577-0889 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Raymond J. Tittman, Esq. of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP (415) 989-5900 appearing for Defendant and moving party. David M. Helbraun, Esq. of Helbraun Law Firm (415) 982-4000 appearing for Defendant Tak Tsui. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. For reasons stated in ruling on Defendant Mid-Century's motion for summary judgment, triable issues of fact raised whether Mid-Century's rescission valid. ASIC does not explain why the issue of whether Mid-Centrury improperly rescinded its policy is relevant to plaintiff's breach of contract claim. Even if Mid-Century improperly rescinded, ASIC has not shown that this would have affect Wells Fargo's actions and requirements with respect to retaining ASIC's insurance for plaintiff's property. The undisputed facts do not show that ASIC's policy was cancelled or otherwise removed based on Mid-Century's purportedly improper rescission. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Lisa A. Costello, of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth, (650) 592-5400, appearing for Defendant and moving party; John N. Frye, Esq. Attorney at Law, (650) 577-0889 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; David M. Helbraun, Esq. of Helbraun Law Firm (415) 982-4000 appearing for Defendant Tak Tsui. (This tentative ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof regarding rescission, and triable issues of fact raised. Memorandum of Fire Insurance is initialed on all pages by both plaintiff and the agent. The term "unrepaired damage" is not explained or defined. Lam described the property as a "fixer upper" which should have prompted further inquiry and investigation. Lam's deposition reflects some lack of knowledge about damage to the property and what should be fixed. (Plaintiffs' UMP #53, 71.) Triable issue of fact regarding the agent's duty to inspect the entire property, (Plaintiffs' UMF 103, Neal deposition) and the agent's checklist (#105, 106). Photos of the property show damage the agent should have reported. (Plaintiffs' UMF 171, Neal deposition.) Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's investigation was proper and led to a "genuine dispute." (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice And Motion To Transfer And Consolidate
Off Calendar Mar-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CAROL LAM, and, LAI LEE'S Motion to Transfer to San Francisco Superior Court the action entitled Farmers Insurance Company, Inc. vs. Carol Sui Man Lam, William Zhou and Lai Fong Lee (RG 08 416808) of the Alameda Superior Court and consolidate with Case CGC-07-468278 of the San Francisco Superior Court, in the County San Francisco, Superior Court of the State of California. Appearances: John N. Frye, Esq., Law Offices of John N. Frye, (650) 577-0889, appearing for Plaintiffs, and moving party; David M. Helbraun, Esq. of the Helbraun Law Firm, (415) 982-4000, appearing for Defendant, Tak Tsui, individually, and Tak Tsui Insurance, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. The transfer will promote the ends of justice. Both cases arise from the same incident. The complaint in the San Francisco case and the cross-complaint in the Alameda case are substantially similar. These actions are not complex. Common question of law and facts is predominating and significant: the condition of the property before the incident, damages, and responsibility for payment of the damages. The parties, witnesses and counsel will not be inconvenienced by the transfer. Both cases are recently filed. Transfer would be in the interest of judicial economy, and eliminate the risk of inconsistent rulings, orders, or judgments. Coordination increases the likelihood of settlement of the actions. Order transferring and consolidating the matter, signed in open Court.(302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
